items.claim: Stage 7e v4 completed a targeted low-cost-model G8/G9 smoke with 4/4 completed and passed runs after retrying one provider timeout.
Stage 7e v1-v4 supports a mechanism-first repair loop for fixed evidence-decision macros, not broad workflow readiness.
Provider execution stability remains a practical uncertainty because Stage 7e v4 required a timeout retry and one truncated retry before success.
The claim-boundary memo was updated to separate task-slice claims, mechanism-composition claims, and unsupported claims.
No supplied evidence supports production readiness, universal model-gap closure, or immediate full workflow expansion.
The next bounded macro should be an evidence-bound method-plan update that reuses Stage 7e v4 obligations before any real-model execution.
The methodology outline defines next macro admission criteria: reuse Stage 7e v4 obligations, add at most one new stressor, run local golden/bad gates first, and declare non-claims.
The backlog marks Stage 7-next as open and keeps full project initialization and full research workflow blocked until the next macro passes local gates and a targeted real-model slice.
